items.briefing:
Australian critical infrastructure operators are being urged to review and tighten router security following warnings about Russian cyber activity targeting network devices. Separately, a surge in phishing toolkits designed to bypass Microsoft 365 multi-factor authentication is putting Australian organisations at risk — attackers can intercept login sessions even when MFA is enabled. Security vendors are also reminding organisations that applying patches quickly matters more than tracking the number of vulnerabilities.

If you use Microsoft 365, be extra cautious with unexpected login prompts or emails asking you to verify your account — when in doubt, contact your IT team before clicking anything.

featured.title: Phishing toolkits target Microsoft 365 MFA in surge
featured.quote: Microsoft 365 MFA attacks are rising, making account takeovers increasingly difficult to prevent for Australian businesses.
